title: NEW P1-19: DOM-based XSS surface detection module
acceptance_criteria: --- MODULE FILE (p1-19-dom-xss.ts) ---
Export: export function runDomXssModule(crawl: CrawlResult): RawFinding[]

GRACEFUL NO-OP: if crawl.loadedChunkContents is absent or empty, return [] immediately — this is the static-fetch fallback path. The function must be byte-identical in behaviour to the pre-feature state when headless rendering was not used.
Scan each chunk in crawl.loadedChunkContents (Record<string, string>) for HIGH-CONFIDENCE sink+source patterns only. The chunk key is a URL string; value is the JS content.
High-confidence patterns to detect (all case-sensitive, use regex with appropriate context window):
Pattern 1: document\.write\s*\(\s*(?:window\.)?location\b — document.write receiving location object or property
Pattern 2: \.innerHTML\s*=\s*[^;\n]{0,80}?(?:location|document\.referrer)\b — innerHTML assigned from location or referrer
Pattern 3: \beval\s*\(\s*(?:window\.)?location\b — eval receiving location
Pattern 4: \bsetTimeout\s*\(\s*(?:window\.)?location\b — setTimeout receiving location as string arg
DO NOT flag generic document.write(variable), generic innerHTML=variable, or eval(variable) without a confirmed external source — too many false positives from framework internals.
For each unique pattern matched in a chunk: emit one RawFinding per distinct pattern match (deduplicate: only one finding per pattern per chunk URL).

Finding shape: moduleId='P1-19', severity='HIGH', confidence='low', category='Client-Side Security', title='Potential DOM-based XSS sink with external source', location=<chunk URL>, evidence=<matched snippet, trimmed to 150 chars>, explanation explains why this specific pattern is dangerous, fixManual gives remediation steps.
Cap total P1-19 findings to 10 (one per chunk+pattern combo). If more patterns match, note the count in the last finding's evidence.
--- REGISTRATION (index.ts) ---
Add import: import { runDomXssModule } from './modules/p1-19-dom-xss';
Call it in Group 2 (synchronous section, after runWebManifestModule): const domXssFindings = runDomXssModule(crawlResult);
Add to allFindings array: { id: 'P1-19', findings: domXssFindings }
--- DISPLAY METADATA (data.ts) ---

Add a ScanModule entry for P1-19 to the SCAN_MODULES array, following the same structure as P1-17 and P1-18 entries. Fields: id='P1-19', name='DOM XSS Surface', description='Detects high-confidence DOM-based XSS sink+source patterns in client-side JavaScript bundles (headless render path only).', category='Client-Side Security'.
--- TESTS ---
Test: crawl.loadedChunkContents absent → empty array returned, no error.
Test: chunk containing 'document.write(location.' → one HIGH finding with confidence=low.
Test: chunk containing 'innerHTML = location.hash' → one HIGH finding.
Test: chunk containing generic 'document.write(myVar)' (no location source) → NO finding.
Test: chunk containing React runtime code with location references → NO false-positive finding.
Test: multiple pattern matches across chunks → findings capped at 10.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
